In [1]:
from cartopy import config
import cartopy.crs as ccrs
import xarray as xr
import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib as mpl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import os
In [2]:
data_crs = ccrs.PlateCarree(central_longitude=0)

# But you want the map to include the dateline.
proj_crs = ccrs.PlateCarree(central_longitude=180)
In [3]:
# eez and country boundaries
eez = gpd.read_file("/media/shilpa/Expansion/for_sophie_18_april_2024/World_EEZ_v11_20191118/eez_v11.shp")
#eez = gpd.read_file("/home/shilpa/Downloads/World_EEZ_v11_20191118/eez_v11.shp")
In [4]:
ncd = eez[eez["TERRITORY1"] == "New Caledonia"]
In [5]:
start_date = '1993-01-01'
end_date = '2023-10-24'

# Create datetime range
date_range = pd.date_range(start=start_date, end=end_date)
print(len(date_range))

11254
In [6]:
# lets get the coastal information
directory = '/media/shilpa/Expansion/for_sophie_18_april_2024/coastal_newcal'
coastal_files = [file for file in os.listdir(directory) if file.startswith("New Caledonia_spatial")]

# Read all CSV files and concatenate into a single DataFrame

# hot season

hot_daily_events = []
hot_lat = []
hot_lon = []

for file in coastal_files:
    # Handle file names with spaces by adding double quotes around the file path
    file_path = os.path.join(directory, file)
    df = pd.read_csv(file_path)

    # use the first value of lat and lon because all values are the same...this file is for only one location
    lat = df['latitude'].iloc[0]
    lon = df['longitude'].iloc[0]
    hot_lat.append(lat)
    hot_lon.append(lon)   
    
    df['time_dt'] = date_range.values
    df['time_dt'] = pd.to_datetime(df['time_dt'])

    # Extract the month component
    df['Month'] = df['time_dt'].dt.month

    # Filter the DataFrame for months November (11) to April (4)
    filtered_df = df[(df['Month'] >= 11) | (df['Month'] <= 4)]

    # Optionally, you can drop the 'Month' column if you don't need it anymore
    filtered_df = filtered_df.drop(columns=['Month'])

    filtered_df_count = filtered_df[filtered_df['spatial_extent'] == 1].copy() # only keep lines with value of 1 for spatail extent
    
    #len(filtered_df_count) # gives the number of days in hot season with MHW
    hot_daily_events.append(len(filtered_df_count))
    
hot_df = pd.DataFrame({'count_hot': hot_daily_events, 'lat':hot_lat, 'lon': hot_lon})

hot_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(hot_df, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(hot_df.lon, hot_df.lat))


# cold season

cold_daily_events = []
cold_lat = []
cold_lon = []

for file in coastal_files:
    # Handle file names with spaces by adding double quotes around the file path
    file_path = os.path.join(directory, file)
    df = pd.read_csv(file_path)

    # use the first value of lat and lon because all values are the same...this file is for only one location
    lat = df['latitude'].iloc[0]
    lon = df['longitude'].iloc[0]
    cold_lat.append(lat)
    cold_lon.append(lon)   
    
    df['time_dt'] = date_range.values
    df['time_dt'] = pd.to_datetime(df['time_dt'])

    # Extract the month component
    df['Month'] = df['time_dt'].dt.month

    # Filter the DataFrame for months November (11) to April (4)
    # Filter the DataFrame for months May (5) to October (10)
    filtered_df = df[(df['Month'] >= 5) & (df['Month'] <= 10)]

    # Optionally, you can drop the 'Month' column if you don't need it anymore
    filtered_df = filtered_df.drop(columns=['Month'])

    filtered_df_count = filtered_df[filtered_df['spatial_extent'] == 1].copy() # only keep lines with value of 1 for spatail extent
    
    #len(filtered_df_count) # gives the number of days in hot season with MHW
    cold_daily_events.append(len(filtered_df_count))
    
cold_df = pd.DataFrame({'count_cold': cold_daily_events, 'lat':cold_lat, 'lon': cold_lon})

cold_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(cold_df, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(cold_df.lon, cold_df.lat))
In [7]:
merged_df = pd.merge(hot_gdf, cold_gdf, on=['lat', 'lon'], how='outer')

# Sum the 'duration' values for matching rows
merged_df['duration_sum'] = merged_df['count_cold'] + merged_df['count_hot']

# Select only the necessary columns (lat, lon, and the sum of duration)
result_df = merged_df[['lat', 'lon', 'duration_sum']]
In [8]:
result_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(result_df, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(result_df.lon, result_df.lat))

result_gdf

In [10]:
directory = '/media/shilpa/Expansion/for_sophie_18_april_2024/coastal_newcal'
coastal_files = [file for file in os.listdir(directory) if file.startswith("New Caledonia_all_mhws_at_")]

mean_duration_ = []
sum_duration_ = []
mean_maxintensity_ = []

lat_ = []
lon_ = []

for file in coastal_files:
    # Handle file names with spaces by adding double quotes around the file path
    file_path = os.path.join(directory, file)
    df = pd.read_csv(file_path)
    print(file_path)
    # use the first value of lat and lon because all values are the same...this file is for only one location
    lat = df['latitude'].iloc[0]
    lon = df['longitude'].iloc[0]
    print(lat)
    print(lon)
    lat_.append(lat)
    lon_.append(lon) 
    mean_maxintensity = df['intensity_max'].mean()
    mean_duration = df['duration'].mean()   
    print(mean_maxintensity)
    print(mean_duration)
    mean_duration_.append(mean_duration)
    mean_maxintensity_.append(mean_maxintensity)
    sum_duration = df['duration'].sum()   
    print(sum_duration)
    sum_duration_.append(sum_duration)

df_= pd.DataFrame({'mean_duration': mean_duration_,'sum_duration': sum_duration_,'mean_maxintensity': mean_maxintensity_, 'lat':lat_, 'lon': lon_})

In [13]:
def easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data,column,i,s, countryboundry ,name,legend_kwds, vmin,vmax,units,ylim,xlim):
    
    norm = mpl.colors.Normalize(vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax)
    cmap ='rainbow' 


    data.plot(column,ax=axs[i],marker = 's',s = s,transform=data_crs,alpha = 1,cmap='rainbow', norm = norm,
            legend=False,legend_kwds=legend_kwds)

    #axs[i].coastlines()

    countryboundry.boundary.plot(ax=axs[i], color="black",alpha = 0.4,transform=data_crs)
    #ncd.boundary.plot(ax=axs[i], color="black",alpha = 0.4,transform=data_crs)
    #vanuatu.boundary.plot(ax=axs[i], color="black",alpha = 0.4,transform=data_crs)
    #fiji.boundary.plot(ax=axs[i], color="black",alpha = 0.4,transform=data_crs)
    #tonga.boundary.plot(ax=axs[i], color="black",alpha = 0.4,transform=data_crs)
    
    sm = plt.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap=cmap, norm=norm)
    sm.set_array([])
    #sm._A = []  # Fake an empty scalar mappable
    cbar = plt.colorbar(sm, ax=axs[i],orientation='horizontal',shrink=0.7)
    cbar.ax.tick_params(labelsize=33) 
    cbar.set_label(units, fontsize=33)
    
    gl = axs[i].gridlines(draw_labels=True)
    gl.top_labels = False
    gl.right_labels = False
    gl.xlabel_style = {'fontsize': 33}
    gl.ylabel_style = {'fontsize': 33}
    axs[i].set_xlim(xlim) 
    axs[i].set_ylim(ylim)
    axs[i].set_title(name, fontsize=35)
In [14]:
cname = 'New_Caledonia' 
 

xlim = (-22, -10.5)
ylim = (-25.4, -17.5)

countryboundry = ncd
    


nrows = 1
ncols = 2
    
fig, axs = plt.subplots(nrows=nrows,ncols=ncols,subplot_kw={'projection': proj_crs},figsize=(34,15))
    

    
easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data=gdf,column= 'mean_maxintensity',
                              name='Mean max intensity',i=0, countryboundry = countryboundry,
                              vmin = gdf.mean_maxintensity.min(),
                              vmax = gdf.mean_maxintensity.max(),s=20,units = "[°C] " ,
                                 ylim = ylim,xlim = xlim , legend_kwds={'label': "[°C] ", 'orientation': "horizontal",
                                                                        'shrink': 0.7})

easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data=gdf,column= 'mean_duration',
                              name='Mean Duration',i=1, countryboundry = countryboundry,
                              vmin = gdf.mean_duration.min(),
                              vmax = gdf.mean_duration.max(),
                               s= 20,units = "[days]" ,ylim = ylim,xlim = xlim , 
                               legend_kwds={'label': "[days]", 'orientation': "horizontal",'shrink': 0.7})

    
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f'/home/shilpa/glory_mat_analysis/coastal_points/{cname}_coast_ines.png',bbox_inches='tight')

cname = 'New_Caledonia' 
 

xlim = (-22, -10.5)
ylim = (-25.4, -17.5)

countryboundry = ncd
    


nrows = 1
ncols = 2
    
fig, axs = plt.subplots(nrows=nrows,ncols=ncols,subplot_kw={'projection': proj_crs},figsize=(34,15))
    
    
easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data=gdf,column= 'mean_maxintensity',
                              name='Mean max intensity',i=0, countryboundry = countryboundry,
                              vmin = gdf.mean_maxintensity.min(),
                              vmax = gdf.mean_maxintensity.max(),s=20,units = "[°C] " ,
                                 ylim = ylim,xlim = xlim , legend_kwds={'label': "[°C] ", 'orientation': "horizontal",
                                                                        'shrink': 0.7})

easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data=gdf,column= 'days_per_year',
                              name='Total MHW days',i=1, countryboundry = countryboundry,
                              vmin = gdf.days_per_year.min(),
                              vmax = gdf.days_per_year.max(),
                               s= 20,units = "[days per year]" ,ylim = ylim,xlim = xlim , 
                               legend_kwds={'label': "[days per year]", 'orientation': "horizontal",'shrink': 0.7})

    
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f'/home/shilpa/glory_mat_analysis/coastal_points/{cname}_coast_ines_.png',bbox_inches='tight')

easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data=gdf,column= 'mean_maxintensity',
                              name='Mean max intensity [GLORYS]',i=0, countryboundry = countryboundry,
                              vmin = gdf.mean_maxintensity.min(),
                              vmax = gdf.mean_maxintensity.max(),s=20,units = "[°C] " ,
                                 ylim = ylim,xlim = xlim , legend_kwds={'label': "[°C] ", 'orientation': "horizontal",
                                                                        'shrink': 0.7})

easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data=gdf,column= 'days_per_year',
                              name='Total MHW days [GLORYS]',i=1, countryboundry = countryboundry,
                              vmin = gdf.days_per_year.min(),
                              vmax = gdf.days_per_year.max(),
                               s= 20,units = "[days per year]" ,ylim = ylim,xlim = xlim , 
                               legend_kwds={'label': "[days per year]", 'orientation': "horizontal",'shrink': 0.7})

    
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f'/home/shilpa/glory_mat_analysis/coastal_points/{cname}_coast_ines_glorys.png',bbox_inches='tight')

easy_plot_unevencolorbars_clims2(data=gdf,column= 'mean_maxintensity',

                              vmin = 1.18,
                              vmax = 2.04,

                                 ylim = ylim,xlim = xlim , legend_kwds={'label': "[°C] ", 'orientation': "horizontal",

                              name='Total MHW days [GLORYS]',i=1, countryboundry = countryboundry,

                               legend_kwds={'label': "[days per year]", 'orientation': "horizontal",'shrink': 0.7})

    
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f'/home/shilpa/glory_mat_analysis/coastal_points/{cname}_coast_glorys.png',bbox_inches='tight')

vmax_int = gdf.mean_maxintensity.max()
vmin_dur = gdf.days_per_year.min()
vmax_dur = gdf.days_per_year.max() 

print(vmin_int,vmax_int,vmin_dur,vmax_dur)
